seoTitle: Crooked Corner vs Croom — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Crooked Corner and Croom. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Crooked Corner vs Croom: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Crooked Corner and Croom.
heading: Crooked Corner vs Croom — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Crooked Corner, New South Wales and Croom, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Crooked Corner has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Croom (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Crooked Corner or Croom safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Crooked Corner has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Croom scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Crooked Corner scores 54/100 for bushfire risk. Croom scores 51/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Crooked Corner or Croom?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Crooked Corner has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
